subject: Human Resources
title: Data Privacy and Employee Records
description: A micro-lesson that makes Data Privacy and Employee Records usable.

hook.text: An HR database is breached. Social security numbers, medical records, and salary data for 10,000 employees are leaked.

buildup.text: Employers hold sensitive data—SSNs, health info, performance reviews, and disciplinary records—requiring careful protection.

discovery.text: HIPAA protects health data. Various state laws regulate how employee personal information must be stored and secured.

twist.text: The U.S. has no comprehensive federal employee data privacy law—unlike the EU's GDPR, which gives workers extensive data rights.

climax.text: Best practices include limiting data collection, securing storage, restricting access, and having breach response plans.

punchline.text: Collecting data is easy. Protecting it is a legal obligation.

quiz.question: What gap exists in U.S. employee data privacy law?
options: There's no comprehensive federal employee data privacy law
